sourceMovie.title: Blazing Saddles
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Blazing Saddles (1974) as a western theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The western and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Ballad of Buster Scruggs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blazing Saddles clicked for you, The Ballad of Buster Scruggs (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in western and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blazing Saddles?
faq.answer: Try The Ballad of Buster Scruggs, Maverick, ¡Three Amigos!—each shares DNA with Blazing Saddles while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blazing Saddles?
faq.answer: Blazing Saddles (1974) is best described as Western, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blazing Saddles worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this western lane.
